content.full_text:
Standish Litchfield Fire Protection District

P.O. Box 120                          Business phone
Standish, CA 96128                    (916) 254-6601


JUN 8 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 600,
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Conflict of interest concerning participation in adopting
    the Standish-Litchfield Fire Protection Districts annual
    Budget.

Dear Sirs:
    In reply to your request for a written letter from Board
of Directors Chairman Arnold Reddoch, concerning his
participation in the adoption of our new 1992-1993 budget.
Mr. Reddoch is concerned with the matter of our line item
#25 (transportation and travel). He does not wish to participate
in approving this line item, as the department has in the past
purchased fuel for our fire equipment from his establishment.
    As it was stated by your office to Mona Ingram the Standish
Litchfield Protection District Secretary, when she called
your office on Mr. Reddochs request, that he should not
participate in any monetary decision that affects this particular
line item #25.

    Mr. Reddoch would appreciate a letter from your office
for his files, and for the Districts files concerning your
decision.

    We are including a photocopy of our expenditure guideline
concerning this item in question, and we are also including
a photocopy of Mr. Reddoch's letter which he received from our
Lassen County Counsel, Mr. Dawson Arnold.

    Thanking you kindly, I am

                    [signature]
                    Arnold Reddoch, Chairman
                    Board of Directors.

COUNTY OF LASSEN

Dawson Arnold
County Counsel

County Administrative Center
707 Nevada Street
Susanville, California 96130
(916) 227-8311, Ext. 334

William Ballew
Deputy County Counsel

May 28, 1992

HAND-DELIVERED

Standish-Litchfield Fire
Protection District
Post Office Box 120
Standish, California 96128

Attention: Mr. Arnold Reddoch, Chairman

RE: Request for Conflict of Interest Opinion

Dear Mr. Reddoch and Members of the Board of Directors:

By hand-delivered letter received May 21, 1992, you request
a written opinion of this office as to whether the purchase of
diesel and gasoline by the District from Wayside Inn, in which
Arnold Reddoch, one of the District Directors, has a proprietary
interest, is a "conflict of interest" prohibited by California law.
Our conclusion is in the negative.

There are two sets of "conflict of interest" provisions in
California law. One such set of provisions, contained in the
Political Reform Act, (Government Code § 81000 et seq.) deals with
disclosure of the interests of public officers and employees and
their disqualification from participating in decisions which would
affect their private interests. The second set of provisions is
contained in what is known as the Corrupt Practices Act,
(Government Code §§ 1090 through 1097, inclusive). Section 1090
provides that public officers or employees shall not be financially
interested in any contract made by them in their official capacity
or by any body or board of which they are members and that such
officers and employees shall not be a vendor at any purchase made
by them in their official capacity.

A list of exceptions is provided to said prohibition in
Government Code § 1091 which reads in pertinent part as follows:

(a) An officer shall not be deemed to be
interested in a contract entered into by a body
or board of which the officer is a member
within the meaning of this article if the
officer has only a remote interest in the

JUN 8 1992

Arnold Reddoch
Page 2
May 28, 1992

contract and if the fact of that interest is
disclosed to the body of the board of which
the officer is a member and noted in its
official records, and thereafter the body or
board authorizes, approves, or ratifies the
contract in good faith, by a vote of its
membership sufficient for the purpose without
counting the vote or votes of the officer or
member with the remote interest.

(b) As used in this article, "remote
interest" means any of the following:

(1)...

(2)...

(8) That of a supplier of goods or
services when those goods or services have been
supplied to the contracting party by the
officer for at least five years prior to his
or her election or appointment to office...

Id.

I have personally reviewed pertinent records in the
office of the Lassen County Clerk which reflect that Virgil A. and
Wanda L. Reddoch and Marvin W. Dye and Anita M. Dye filed a
statement of doing business under the fictitious name "Wayside Inn"
on April 15, 1981. The County Clerk's records for public officers
within the County of Lassen reflect that Arnold Reddoch was
appointed to the office of Director of the Standish-Litchfield Fire
Protection District on November 14, 1989. Thus, as Mr. Reddoch has
been a supplier of goods to the District for more than five years
prior to his assumption of office, his interest is defined as a
"remote interest" and the District may continue to purchase its
fuel from the Wayside Inn.

As stated above, Mr. Reddoch should not participate in
approval of any bill or warrant which involves the purchase of fuel
from the Wayside Inn, as he is disqualified under the provisions
of Government Code § 87100 ("no public official at any level of
state or local government shall make, participate in making, or in
any way attempt to use his official position to influence a
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he
has a financial interest.") Mr. Reddoch must also ensure that his
abstention, and the reason for it, is duly noted in the minutes of
the board meeting as required by § 1091(a).

Arnold Reddoch
Page 3
May 28, 1992

I recommend that Mr. Reddoch contact directly the Fair
Political Practices Commission at 428 J Street, Suite 600,
Sacramento, California 95814, (916) 322-5660, and request advise
from that body as to whether he may participate in the adoption
of the annual district budget (as the budget doubles includes a
line item for "fuel" which might reasonably be expected to be
purchased from his business. This office can offer no advice on
that question as the "advise of counsel" defense in conflict of
interest matters lies solely within the jurisdiction of the FPPC,
and arises only from formal opinions issued by it.

One final comment is in order. Your letter notes that Mr.
Reddoch has been appointed Board Chairman. While I have no
information on what is the procedure in the Standish-Litchfield
Fire Protection District by-laws of some districts provide that the
Chairman signs various official documents including minutes and or
warrants. Other districts have all the members of the board sign
minutes as approved. In any event, the act of affixing the
signature of the Chair, or President of the Board, is ministerial
in nature only and it is the opinion of this office that the Courts
would not regard the act of signing papers as either "participating
in a decision" or as entering into a contract with himself within
the meaning of either the Political Reform Act or Corrupt Practices
Law.

I sincerely trust that this letter will be dispositive of the
matter, but if further information is desired, please do not
hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

COUNTY COUNSEL
COUNTY OF LASSEN

[signature]
DAWSON ARNOLD,
County Counsel

DA:lh


Arnold
file
10                                EXPENDITURES                                    June
                                                                                   1992

25. Transportation and travel   Includes the transportation of persons and
    things, except where other services furnished in conjunction with
    transportation comprise the major portion of the charge, or where the
    transportation is more appropriately included under subobject titles
    indicating purpose.

    Types of items included:
        Central garage services
        Delivery services
        Freight, express and other transportation charges
            not chargeable to a commodity purchased
        Gasoline, diesel fuel, except when purchased for
            central garage
        Leased or hired vehicles
        Reimbursement for private car use
        Reimbursement for meals, lodging, conference
            registration, bridge tolls; train, bus, air
            fares; any other authorized travel expense
        Transportation of prisoners and court wards

    Types of items excluded:
        Ambulance service - Post to "Professional and
            specialized services".
        Armored car service - Post to "Professional
            and specialized services".
        Packing supplies - Post to "Special departmental
            expense".
        Transportation of indigents - Post to "Support
            and care of persons".
        Purchases for central garage vehicles - Post to
            "Maintenance-equipment" or "Special departmental
            expense".
        Gasoline, diesel fuel, etc. used for off-highway
            purposes by Road Department - Post to "Maintenance-equipment".
        Natural gas, butane, fuel oil, etc. used for heating/cooling purposes - Post to "Utilities".

26. Utilities

    Types of items included:
        Coal                          Natural gas, butane, fuel oil
        Electricity                   Sewage disposal
        Heating and cooling           Street lighting on county
        Supplies for county           grounds
            buildings                 Water

    Types of items excluded:
        Refuse disposal - Post to "Household expense".
        Telephone service - Post to "Communications".


3/77

sections.question_synthetic: The Standish-Litchfield Fire Protection District seeks FPPC written confirmation regarding Board Chairman Arnold Reddoch's conflict of interest concerning his participation in approving the district's annual budget, particularly line item #25 (transportation and travel), given that the district has purchased fuel from Reddoch's business (Wayside Inn).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request to FPPC (not an FPPC response letter), plus supporting documentation including a county counsel opinion. The county counsel opinion provides analysis but explicitly states that FPPC formal advice is needed. No Q/C sections in the incoming request letter itself. Confidence reduced due to document being a compilation of materials rather than a single coherent FPPC advice letter.

embedding.summary: This is a compilation of correspondence from the Standish-Litchfield Fire Protection District to the FPPC requesting written advice on a conflict of interest matter, along with a prior opinion letter from Lassen County Counsel concluding that Reddoch's interest is 'remote' under Government Code §1091(b)(8), but recommending direct FPPC consultation regarding budget participation under the Political Reform Act.
